pattern: A difference-array sweep over locations tracks net occupancy so you can verify capacity is never exceeded at any point along the route.
hints: Passengers board at one location and leave at another, so each trip is a +delta at pickup and a -delta at dropoff.
If you record those changes per kilometer and accumulate them in order, the running total is the car's occupancy.
Build a timeline keyed by location, add passengers at 'from' and subtract at 'to', then sweep left to right; if the running sum ever exceeds capacity return false.

edge_inputs.description: same trips fit with one more seat
dropoff and pickup at the same location must not overlap
back-to-back non-overlapping trips reuse the seats
two simultaneous trips sum over capacity

stress.description: many random short trips over a bounded route with a moderate capacity
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    trips = []
    for _ in range(n):
        a = rng.randrange(0, 1000)
        b = rng.randrange(a + 1, 1001)
        p = rng.randrange(1, 101)
        trips.append([p, a, b])
    capacity = rng.randrange(1, 100001)
    return [trips, capacity]
stress.note: wide capacity range produces both true and false cases
